project: University of East Anglia (UEA) Diamondback and SAN configs

vendor.note: The review refers to IBM configuration outputs and IBM-branded line items, but does not explicitly name the contracting supplier.

quote_source.value: Diamondback TXT output and SAN switch TXT output
quote_source.note: Appendix B and Appendix C describe the TXT output as the canonical human-readable config reference. The raw TXT files were not directly included in the prepared bundle.

revision_or_date.note: The supplied review file name includes v6 and the document title includes 23 April 2026.

subtotal.note: Calculated as combined hardware purchase GBP 3,334,024.40 plus shipping and handling GBP 10,014.32 across Diamondback and SAN. No explicit formal subtotal is shown in the bundle.

vat.note: No VAT figure is shown in the prepared bundle.

total.note: No explicit grand total is shown. The strongest defensible inferred pre-VAT one-time total is the same as the subtotal above: GBP 3,344,038.72 including shipping.

lead_time.note: No lead time is shown in the prepared bundle.

inclusions: Two Diamondback systems with identical structure, including Diamondback Tape Library 3yr, 10 x LTO10 FH Fibre Channel Drive per system, Full Capacity License, power/PDU items, diagnostic cartridge, cleaning cartridge, preloaded media enablement kit, LTO Service Magazine, warranty/maintenance items, and shipping/handling.

Four SAN switch systems with the same structure, including IBM Storage Networking SAN24B-7, 2 x long-wave optics, 1 x 8-port 32Gbps short-wave SFP bundle, 1 x 8-port 32Gbps short-wave upgrade, 10 x OM3 LC/LC 10m cables, support items, and shipping/handling.
Diamondback overall monthly maintenance evidenced at GBP 2,900.54.
exclusions: 300 LTO-10 cartridges are requested in the email trail but are not visibly evidenced in the reviewed Diamondback config output.
A single formal quoted subtotal or grand total is not shown.
The prepared bundle does not include a spreadsheet or the raw TXT pricing/config files themselves.
assumptions: The supplied review document is treated as the controlling evidence because it is the only directly supplied source file in the prepared bundle.

The Diamondback TXT and SAN switch TXT outputs are treated as the most likely source-of-truth pricing references because the review explicitly describes TXT as the canonical human-readable config reference.
IBM is treated as the likely vendor/manufacturer, not a confirmed contracting supplier.
The subtotal is inferred from appendix totals and is not explicitly stated as a formal commercial subtotal in the bundle.
risks: The email trail asks for 8 LTO10 drives per library, but the reviewed Diamondback config shows 10 drives per library.
The requested 300 LTO-10 cartridges are not visibly evidenced in the supplied Diamondback config output.
The Diamondback package naming says 8xLTO10-FC while the visible config content shows 10 drives per system.
The SAN switch side is broadly plausible, but the visible lines do not conclusively prove the requested 12 x short-wave optics populated state.
The email trail states all configs were for BP review prior to quoting, so the material may have been pre-quote rather than final.
flags: Only the review DOCX was directly supplied in the prepared bundle; underlying TXT/CSV/CFR/XML outputs were referenced but not directly provided.
Lead time, VAT, and explicit grand total were not evidenced.
source_files_used: /root/.openclaw/dropbox/inbox/UEA/Diamondback/UEA Diamondback Config Sanity Check Review v6.docx
